name: Spaghetti Sauce for Canning
recipeIngredient: 8 quarts plum or paste tomatoes, diced
recipeInstructions:
1. Dip tomatoes a few at a time in boiling water for 30 seconds, then place in cool water. The skins will slip right off.
2. Chop coarsely and put in an enamelware or stainless pot big enough to hold the 8 quarts of tomatoes and other ingredients. Add spices and bring to a boil, simmering about 10 minutes. Substitute fresh herbs/spices if you have them. Adjust for personal taste.
3. Mince the garlic and coarsely chop the onion and bell peppers; sauté until just soft in the oil.
4. Add to the tomato mixture and simmer another 10 minutes or so. Do not overcook; remember, there will be further cooking during the canning process and when reheating at final use.
5. Prepare canning jars per manufacturer's instructions. It is now recommended that when canning tomato products additional acid be added. My canner instructions call for 2 tablespoons of bottled (not fresh) lemon juice per quart of product. Put this in the jar before the sauce. I also add 1 teaspoon salt per quart.
6. Process in a pressure canner as directed.
This recipe produces enough sauce for 7 quarts and enough leftovers for one plate of pasta for the cook!
